title: International Relations and South Asia
further_info: Recommended Reading • Kanti P Bajpai and Harsh V Pant, India’s Foreign Policy (New Delhi: Oxford University Press, 2013). • Hedley Bull, the Anarchical Society (New York: Columbia University Press, 1977). • Sumit Ganguly and S Paul Kapur, India, Pakistan and the Bomb (New York: Columbia University Press, 2010). • Sumit Ganguly, ed., Engaging the World: Indian Foreign Policy Since 1947 (New Delhi: Oxford University Press, 2016). • Baldev Raj Nayar and T V Paul, India in the World Order (New York: Cambridge University Press, 2003). • Kenneth A Oye, ed., Cooperation under Anarchy (Princeton: Princeton University Press, 1986). • John G Ruggie, ed., Multilateralism Matters (New York: Columbia University Press, 1993). • Jack L Snyder, From Voting to Violence (New York: Norton, 2000). • Kenneth N Waltz, Theory of International Politics (New York: McGraw-Hill, 1979). • Alexander Wendt, Social Theory of International Politics (New York: Cambridge University Press, 1999).
